about.text: An engineer with a genuine passion for data, systems, and the craft of building and testing reliable software. I make sure your product has the highest standards of quality in the market. Outside of the terminal I search for interesting people and experiences.
A curious human with experience facilitating group experiences and help designning ecosystems that nurture soils, souls and society.
There are a lot of things I would love to do. Some I have already done and would like to keep improving — this is how I keep track.

bullets: Worked for AI Training Data industry as contractor
Multi-Modal Quality Assurance: Evaluated AI-generated text, images, and structured data to identify factual errors, hallucinations, and inconsistencies across diverse content categories.
Technical Testing & Code Review: Debugged AI-generated code and diagnosed logic errors and edge cases.
Model Optimization & Reporting: Technical documentation for AI model refinement.
I spent spring and summer of 2026 volunteering in eco villages in Portugal.
I learned about permaculture design, decentralized governance, community
Ten Lives is the first regenrative festival in europe
Participants work in the land in the morning, have conferences in the afternoon and party at night
My responsibility was to guide teams of particiapants into the regeneration work and leading teams into the work
Ensured end-to-end quality of the NDCX NDC API platform by defining and executing test plans within a SAFe/Scrum team.
Performed exploratory, functional, and end-to-end testing of airline, GDS and payment integrations via APIs and UI flows.
Designed automated regression tests in Python (internal framework + Robot Framework); reviewed C++ changes to assess testing impact.
Led design and implementation of the CI/CD testing pipeline (Jenkins, BIRD), integrating automated suites into deployments.
Managed user stories, test cases, and defects in Jira and Octane; mentored juniors and contributed to team-wide testing standards.
Functional, integration, and regression testing on embedded software systems for automotive ECUs.
Testing platform for Autonomous Driving (BMW / AUTOSAR) using TDFNext Framework with Hardware-in-Loop (HiL) simulation.
Stack size management for automotive embedded systems — integration of Gliwa T1 for runtime measurements.
Agile environment; collaborated across developers, project managers, and clients to report testing progress and risks.

experience.org: Institute of Systems and Robotics, Univ. Coimbra
bullets: Applied computer vision and machine learning to recognise and authenticate images using advanced image descriptors.
Technology creates unique stochastic markings to authenticate high-end products — currently deployed in tobacco package stamps.
Extracted forensic features (particles, faces, fingerprints) for use in identity documents. Collaboration with Portugal's National Mint (INCM).
Organised all logistics for trips and events across Portugal for international students — accommodations, transport, activities.
Provided training and onboarding for new members.

experience.org: Board of European Students of Technology (BEST)
bullets: Strategic communication between BEST and its stakeholders: university, companies, students, and media.
Social media content creation, event planning, financial oversight, and organisational governance.
Browser automation with no-code tools and Python, applying ISTQB techniques to ensure optimal web app functionality.
QA strategy design, Agile methodologies, and efficient bug tracking.
USed Selenium, Cypress and Playwright for web
Created and managed marketing campaigns, social media content, and website copywriting.
SPEAK is a non-profit organization focused on breaking language barriers between communities by providing a platform for easier integration.
Developed front-end and back-end features on their web application using the OutSystems Platform.
UI improvements in HTML and CSS; collaborated with multidisciplinary teams on project planning.

education.note: Computer Architecture, Computer Vision, Digital Fabrication, MEMS, High Performance Computing, Real-Time Systems, Systems Engineering.
Comprehensive engineering sciences with hands-on laboratory and project-oriented learning.
Secondary Education Diploma – Science and Technology

education.school: Corpo Nacional de Escutas — National Body of the Scout Movement
education.note: Non-formal education in the Scout Movement rooted in nature, service to local communities, leadership development, outdoor survival, constructive dialogue, and values-driven action.

hobbies.name: Surfing
hobbies.note: Ocean lover. Not sure it likes me back.

hobbies.name: Writing & Journaling
hobbies.note: Daily journaling practice. Reflective writing, life audits, occasional existential plot twists.

hobbies.name: Reading
hobbies.note: Philosophy, tech, travel literature, and fiction. Trying to outread my own confusion.

hobbies.name: Camping & Outdoors
hobbies.note: 14 years as a scout. Mountains, forests, survival skills, and questionable cooking experiments.

hobbies.name: Traveling
hobbies.note: Backpack over suitcase, always. Collecting cultures more than souvenirs.

hobbies.name: Diving
hobbies.note: Exploring the underwater world and ‘why am I breathing like Darth Vader?’

hobbies.name: Snowboarding
hobbies.note: Sliding down frozen mountains on purpose.

hobbies.name: Beach Volley
hobbies.note: My way of relaxing at the beach.

hobbies.name: Bouldering
hobbies.note: Solving rock puzzles one fall at a time.

international.title: Volunteering and Traveling in Latin America
international.note: Travelled solo across South America for a full year with a backpack. Immersed in local cultures, languages, and landscapes. Volunteered in hostels, selling crepes to pay my rent in Colombia, going on a ayahausca retreat and hiking the tallest mountain on earth were some of my adventures. Learned to be more humble apretiate family and friends more and discovered myself.

international.title: Backpacking in southeast asia to learn new skills
international.note: This trip was all about learning new skills and imerse into new realities. Snowboarding in Japan, diving in the Philippines, surfing in Sri Lanka, discovering monks in Thailand and meeting the harsh reality of India with its delicious food.

international.title: Summer courses from the Board of European Students of Technology
international.note: Studying a technology topic with a group of 30 strangers in a European city. Self-driving cars in Ljubljana and Entrepreneurship in Skopje.

international.title: Working and Living in a country I don't speak the language for 3 years
international.note: Living and working abroad in a SaaS multinational company in the travel industry. I learned a new language and the diffulties of moving to a country you don't know the language and know very few people.

bullets: Maresia is minimalist travel agency focused on community.
Our first event is a 6 day road trip on the southwest coast of Portugal, taking a group of people in a 9 seater van and caping at different spots

projects.name: Robot Telemetry Data App
projects.description: Application that records telemetry data from mobile robots via TCP/IP connections.

projects.org: Board of European Students of Technology (BEST)
bullets: Pan-European student engineering competition. Teams of 4 solve complex technical challenges across local, regional, and European finals.

items.title: Get Open Water certification
items.note: Learned to dive in Malapascua, the Philippines.

items.title: Get Advanced Open Water certification
items.note: Got my advanced certification in Gili T, Lombok, Indonesia.

items.title: Diving in Komodo and Raj Ampat, Indonesia
items.note: Diving in one of the most beautiful places on earth — something I would love to do.

items.title: Surf on all continents
items.note: Missing the Americas, Africa, and Oceania.

items.title: Learn to kitesurf
items.note: Flying with a board is a childhood dream never fulfilled.

items.title: Learn to skate surf
items.note: Land training for the ocean.

items.title: Learn how to sail
items.note: A boat and an open horizon. Eventually sail in the Caribbean.

items.note: Learned in France

items.title: Snowboard in Japan
items.note: Accomplished two months ago.

items.note: Feel the wind, see the world from above.

items.note: Northern lights somewhere wild.

items.title: Walk the Camino de Santiago
items.note: Did the Portuguese route. Changed something in me.

items.title: Walk the Camino again
items.note: A different route, starting from Eastern Europe.

items.title: Do a silent retreat in Nepal
items.note: Vipassana or similar. 10 days of silence.

items.title: Do a road trip with a van and a dog
items.note: Experience solitude, reflection, and love while discovering the world.

items.title: Learn to play guitar
items.note: Self-taught. More songs than Wonderwall.

items.note: Words and melody, start to finish.

items.note: One song, played properly.

items.note: Conversations worth recording.

items.title: Become fluent in Spanish
items.note: A year in South America took care of that.

items.note: Living in Nice got me to a conversational level — though jokes are still a work in progress.

items.note: One is a start. Two is a habit.

items.title: Introduction to Chinese language and culture
items.note: The dream is to one day speak all the official languages of the UN.

items.title: Try ayahuasca
items.note: One of the most transformative experiences of my life.

items.note: In progress. Christianity, Islam, Hinduism, Judaism, Agnostics, Atheists, and more to come.

items.note: In progress. Missing Oceania.
